critic_raw: Issue 1: Prognosis ≠ treatment benefit. Issue 2: No cross-tumor evidence. Issue 3: Single-center scope insufficient for slide's claim. Issue 4: Retrospective treatment confounding.
defender_raw: All four issues are valid and require correction of the slide's language. The model's contribution is prognostic enrichment in its specific studied context. Concede the critique on all four issues.

debate_rounds.critic: I note that the defender's concession is complete and appropriate. To be precise: the prognostic value itself is not under dispute — a model that outperforms standard covariates for progression-free survival prediction in its training setting is a scientific contribution. The dispute is strictly about the slide's extension of that contribution to therapy guidance and all solid tumors. Those two extensions are unsupported.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed precisely. The prognostic result stands; the slide's extensions do not. The corrected claim would be: 'This model improves prognostic stratification beyond standard covariates for early-stage breast cancer at our institution. Further prospective validation and separate studies are needed before any therapy-selection or cross-tumor claims can be made.' That is the defensible statement.

adjudication_raw: Both parties agree on all issues. The debate converged quickly because the defender conceded in the initial response. The draft slide's claim is not supported. Verdict: critique_wins.

empirical_test.condition: Prospective multi-center studies estimating treatment-benefit heterogeneity within each tumor type
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: No treatment-benefit heterogeneity; poor cross-tumor transfer
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Prognostic value confirmed broadly; no therapy-selection value demonstrated
